item.title: Apple holiday gift guide: Best products to buy for the 2023 season
item.itunes:title: Apple holiday gift guide: Best products to buy for the 2023 season
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
The holiday season is right around the corner, and now's the time to start buying your loved ones some gifts. Apple products happen to be a prevalent option among many, due to their premium status, popularity, and high resale value. Whether you're seeking one of the latest iPhones, a versatile iPad, a capable Mac, or something else, we've got you covered. We will be highlighting below some of the best Apple product deals that act as solid holiday season gifts.

  • Apple Gift Card
    Best overall
    $25 at Amazon
  • Apple AirTag
    Best for affordability
    $29 at Amazon
  • Apple Watch Series 9
    Best for parents
    $390 at Amazon
  • MacBook Air (M2)
    Best for some special: Productivity
    $949 at Best Buy (13 inches)
  • Apple AirPods Pro 2
    Best for young adults
    $230 at Amazon
  • Apple iPhone 13
    Best for someone special: Medium budget
    $599 at Apple
  • Apple iPad 10
    Best for students
    $399 at Amazon
  • Source: Apple
    Apple iPhone 15 Pro Max
    Best for someone special: Generous edition
    $1100 at Best Buy

Best Apple products to gift

Apple Gift Card
Best overall

Versatile and convenient

The Apple Gift Card is a versatile present that can cost as little as $25. Your friends and family will be able to spend it however they want inside the Apple ecosystem, whether on hardware or software.

Pros
  • Flexible pricing
  • Eco-friendly
  • Supports hardware and software purchases
Cons
  • Apple ID and Gift Card regions must match
  • May be perceived as a boring gift
  • Not as personal as other options
$25 at Amazon $25 at Apple

If you're not sure what your friend or family member likes, but you know they're in the Apple ecosystem, an Apple Gift Card can act as a flexible present. You can gift somebody $25-$200 in Apple credits — between $25, $50, $75, $100, $150, $200, or a custom amount, and you also get to select the number of cards. So, if you want to pay someone more than $200, you could send multiple ones.

You can add a personalized message to your card, so it's not just a generic email when it reaches their inbox. Apple also allows you to choose one of several different patterns, so you can match their vibe. What's great about this digital gift is that you can schedule it beforehand, and it'll arrive whenever you want it to. So if you tend to be forgetful during this season, you can buy it right now and have it delivered to them on Christmas.

So what can they do with Apple credits once they redeem them? Anything related to Apple, whether it be Apple hardware or digital goods like apps, games, subscriptions, and more. The credits also work in both physical Apple Stores and the online one. Though, make sure their Apple ID is based in the same region as the gift card. If you buy a U.S. Apple Gift Card and send it to a foreign friend abroad, it won't work with their non-U.S. Apple ID.

Apple Gift Cards are truly the best for their versatility. Your beloved ones can use them however they want, and you won't have to worry about spending too much if you're on a limited budget. They're also eco-friendly, considering they're delivered as an email and don't go through production and packaging processes.

Apple AirTag
Best for affordability

Small yet effective

Apple's AirTag offers a low-cost, reliable solution when you need to track an object. It integrates into the Find My network, which allows it to be detected by millions of other Apple devices.

Pros
  • Find My network integration
  • Long battery life
  • Affordable
Cons
  • Keychain sold separately
  • Can't pair with Android
  • Scratches easily
$29 at Amazon $29 at Apple $29 at Best Buy

An Apple AirTag is the best affordable, useful Apple gift. It costs a mere $29, and its replaceable battery lasts for a whole year. It's the perfect gift to buy for a distant relative or friend with whom you want to share the joy of the season. An AirTag is also a great option if you're somebody who can't afford to buy your loved ones more expensive gifts.

With Find My Network support, an AirTag can be found if a compatible Apple device passes next to it. So it has better coverage than rivals, such as Tile. AirTags can be used to find anything and everything you might lose. Though, you will need an iPhone or iPad to set it up. So avoid gifting it to Android users.

The best part about AirTags is that you can never have enough of them. They can be attached to keys, backpacks, luggage, wallets, or even cars. Numerous people have found their stolen cars thanks to it, and many users utilize it to remember their parking spots. Unfortunately, though, you will need to separately buy a keychain or case to attach them to objects. Furthermore, their exterior shows scratches quite easily.

These coin-sized tracking devices only come in one color, but there are plenty of colorful attachment accessories that can make them look more fun. You can also craft a keychain accessory to go with your AirTag gift, making it more personal. AirTags also come in a pack of four for a discounted $99. So you can get people more than one if you have a higher budget.

Apple Watch Series 9
Best for parents

24/7 health and fitness monitoring

$390 $399 Save $9

Apple's Watch Series 9 packs in a new, more powerful processor, a brighter display capable of up to 2,000 nits peak brightness, Apple's U2 chip that enables precision-finding of your iPhone, as well as better HomePod integration, and a whole slew of features and tech you'd expect.

Pros
  • Tracks a wide range of health and workout metrics
  • Supports calls, texts, music playback, and more
  • Offers plenty of color options
Cons
  • Mediocre battery life
  • Inferior sleep tracking
  • Requires an iPhone to set up
$390 at Amazon $399 at Best Buy $399 at Apple

Whether it's a parent or a grandparent, the Apple Watch Series 9 is the best gift for them. This smartwatch was released back in September, so it's still a fresh, new product, and there's a lot to like.

The watch is available in so many configurations, so you will likely find at least one combo that matches their style. They'll also get access to a number of health features. So if you're concerned about their health, getting them an Apple Watch would be a great idea. There's a reason why the Apple Watch is the best-selling smartwatch right now. The technology it packs in its compact body is unrivaled.

The Apple Watch Series 9 supports:

  • Blood oxygen measurements
  • ECGs
  • Walking steadiness notifications
  • Cardio fitness level notifications
  • Irregular heart rhythm notifications
  • High & low heart rate notifications
  • Fall detection
  • Emergency SOS
  • Medical ID
  • Loud noise notifications
  • Crash detection
  • Sleep tracking (albeit not very accurate)

If they don't own an iPhone for setup, you don't have to worry about that either. The GPS + Cellular model of the Apple Watch Series 9 can be set up through your iPhone. Though, if neither you nor any of their family members uses an iPhone, then they won't be able to set it up at all.

The health features on this watch aren't solely what makes it so great. If your parent tends to miss calls or notifications, the Apple Watch will make sure this never happens again. Not only does it vibrate on their wrist, but it also can play optional notification sounds. They'll never miss a call or text as long as they wear it. They can even answer calls right from their wrist, so if the phone isn't nearby, they can answer without moving an inch. They can also ask Siri to send texts, so they don't even have to type or click buttons.

The Apple Watch Series 9 starts at $399, which makes it a good gift that's not way too expensive. It also comes with a three-month Apple Fitness Plus trial, so your parent can have somebody guide them through workouts — even ones that are targeted at older adults.

MacBook Air (M2)
Best for some special: Productivity

The best MacBook Air yet

$949 $1099 Save $150

The latest MacBook Air offers the M2 chip, and a redesigned chassis with MagSafe 3 support. It is powerful and can handle pretty much any kind of work, and it comes in either 13.6-inch or 15.3-inch sizes.

Pros
  • Lightweight, sleek design
  • Long battery life
  • Capable M2 chip
Cons
  • Limited port options
  • Somewhat expensive
  • Dull finishes
$949 at Best Buy (13 inches) $1049 at Best Buy (15 inches) $1080 at Amazon (13 inches) $1099 at Amazon (15 inches)

If you're looking to buy a special somebody an expensive gift, you may want to consider a MacBook Air M2. It was released in 2022, making it the latest and most powerful MacBook Air to date. The base model starts at $1,099, so it's not the cheapest holiday gift. But if you want to spend a notable amount on a productivity device for someone you love, you're in the right place.

The MacBook Air M2 offers a fresh exterior design that introduces a notched display in the process. It features two USB-C ports, a MagSafe 3 port, and a 3.5mm headphone jack. I'm personally a big fan of its design because it's clean, slim, and modern, and it comes in four aluminum finishes: Midnight, Starlight, Space Gray, and Silver. While the four colors are somewhat dull, you could always protect it with a case or throw a skin on it. It's still the greatest Apple device for lightweight productivity.

This MacBook starts with 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD. As the name suggests, it features the M2 processor, so you'll get every single new feature of the new macOS Sonoma operating system.

Whether your loved one is a student or someone who works in a field that doesn't require ginormous computing powers, this MacBook will handle their everyday tasks smoothly. It's also safe to assume that it will receive software updates for at least four more years, based on Apple's history. In a nutshell, it's a capable, future-proof device that your beloved will appreciate.

Apple AirPods Pro 2
Best for young adults

A must-have for iPhone users

$230 $249 Save $19

Despite their compact build, the AirPods Pro 2 pack in plenty of features, including active noise cancelation (ANC), Dolby Atmos content, and wireless charging.

Pros
  • Effective ANC
  • Find My integration
  • Spatial Audio support
Cons
  • No companion app on Android
  • Case scratches easily
  • Not the cheapest earbuds
$230 at Amazon $250 at Best Buy $249 at Apple

The AirPods have always been great earbuds, but the AirPods Pro 2 are my favorite of the whole lineup. These true wireless earbuds from Apple offer active noise cancelation (ANC), a feature I use almost daily. Whether you're working at a café, commuting, or merely jogging, ANC really helps you hone in on whatever you're listening to without having to worry about outside noise. There are other AirPods on the market, but the regular models don't support ANC and the AirPods Max are over-ear, which some people are not fans of.

Apple's AirPods Pro 2 also support Spatial Audio with dynamic head tracking. So you get to listen to music the way the artist visualized it when it was getting recorded. This feature is available on Apple Music and some other third-party services. It also works with the Apple TV app, so you can become part of the (supported) movies and series you're watching.

These wireless earbuds are water and sweat-resistant, so you can work out with them. Apple doesn't cover water damage under its limited warranty, so be careful when you're jogging during rainy days. To ensure these little buds don't fall out of your ears when running, check the other ear tip sizes included in the package. You can also do a fit test from your iPhone to make sure you've chosen the correct size.

Apple offers optional engraving when purchasing AirPods from its online store. So if you want to add a unique touch to make your gift more personal, you can do that for free. They're available for $249 in white color only. Their charging case supports Qi and MagSafe wireless chargers, as well as the Apple Watch charging puck.

Apple iPhone 13
Best for someone special: Medium budget

Solid and doesn't cost a fortune

The iPhone 13 is solid for those on a limited budget, offering a dual camera system, A15 Bionic chip, and vibrant color options.

Pros
  • Supports the latest iOS 17
  • Packs the capable A15 Bionic
  • Vibrant finishes
Cons
  • No Dynamic Island
  • 60Hz screen, no AOD
  • Lightning port
$599 at Apple $630 at Best Buy

If you have an extra special person in your life but don't want to spend over a grand, you might want to consider an iPhone 13. This iPhone starts at $599, so it's neither the cheapest nor the most expensive out there. This is because it was released in 2021, so it's not from the latest lineup, but with that age comes that discount.

The iPhone 13 is available in six different finishes: Red, Starlight, Midnight, Blue, Green, and Pink. The base model has 128GB of storage, but it can be upgraded to up to 512GB. Despite being older, it's still powered by Apple's A15 Bionic chip, which is one of the fastest you can get on a smartphone today. It's also the same processor included in the regular iPhone 14 models.

This iPhone has an OLED 6.1-inch Super Retina XDR display for easy carry and a 2532 x 1170 resolution at 460 pixels per inch for vivid, accurate colors. Its display also supports True Tone technology, which adds a warm layer to the colors depending on the surrounding lighting. This makes the colors look more natural and as they're meant to be. This iPhone also has splash, water, and dust resistance, rated at IP68, so it can last at a maximum depth of 6 meters for 30 minutes. However, it's worth reminding you that Apple's limited warranty doesn't cover water damage.

When it comes to the front camera, the iPhone 13 supports Face ID and Portrait Mode camera effects, thanks to the TrueDepth camera system. The rear bump includes two camera lenses that support 4K video recording, Cinematic Mode, Night Mode shooting, and more.

In a nutshell, the iPhone 13 is the best gift to get someone who is exceptionally special to you. It might not be the latest and greatest iPhone, but it'll save you some money and make your loved one feel great this holiday.

Apple iPad 10
Best for students

A low-cost, premium tablet

$399 $449 Save $50

The iPad 10 introduces a complete chassis overhaul and offers four bold colors. It also packs the A14 Bionic chip and supports the Apple Pencil 1 and USB-C.

Pros
  • Supports the latest iPadOS 17
  • USB-C port
  • Sleek design
Cons
  • No Apple Pencil 2 support
  • Dated A14 Bionic processor
  • 60Hz screen
$399 at Amazon $449 at Best Buy $449 at Apple

The affordable iPad 10 was released back in 2022, and it's most suitable for people who don't require a Mac, such as students. While its power is somewhat limited, considering it's a mobile device, iPadOS 17 still makes it a solid productivity tool. It starts at $449 and is available in four finishes: Blue, Pink, Yellow, and Silver.

The iPad 10 comes with a sleek design similar to that of higher-end models. It features thin front bezels without a physical home button. It's a slim, lightweight device that anyone can just slip into their backpack and carry on the go. So whether you're gifting it to a high school or college student, it'll be a decent tablet for paperless education. It's also great for older adults who might not need a dedicated laptop.

The iPad 10 is excellent for attending online classes, taking notes, reading eBooks, listening to podcasts, and watching TV content. It has a 10.9-inch display that supports Apple Pencil 1 and Apple Pencil (USB-C) for drawing or taking notes. The best part? Apple's Notes app recognizes handwriting, so students can search through their notes as if they've been typed on a keyboard.

The front 12MP ultra-wide camera on the iPad has a 122-degree field of view. So if they're moving around while attending a class or a regular video call, the camera will follow them around. However, it's worth mentioning that this camera doesn't support Portrait Mode camera effects or Face ID, due to the lack of a TrueDepth camera system.

Source: Apple
Apple iPhone 15 Pro Max
Best for someone special: Generous edition

The latest and greatest

The iPhone 15 Pro Max introduces an all-new, customizable Action button, a titanium chassis with round edges, and a USB Type-C port. It exclusively offers up to 5x optical zoom, too.

Pros
  • USB-C port
  • Light, titanium build
  • Action button, Dynamic Island, AOD
Cons
  • Costly
  • Dull colorways
  • Too large for some users
$1100 at Best Buy $1200 at Amazon $1199 at Apple

This one is for all of you extra-generous peeps who are looking to buy something fancy. The iPhone 15 Pro Max starts at $1,199 for the base model, making it the most expensive gift in our guide. However, this is the perfect gift if you want to spend a significant amount of money on your loved one or want to get them the latest, highest-end device.

This iPhone comes in four finishes: Black Titanium, White Titanium, Blue Titanium, and Natural Titanium. It has a Ceramic Shield front, textured matte glass back, and a titanium design that makes it look as premium and solid as it gets. While it's one of the most durable iPhones manufactured in the past years, it still features glass fronts and backs, so certain drops might crack them. Repairing the front or back of an iPhone 15 Pro Max costs a fortune, so you may want to invest in a case to minimize potential damage. It would also make for another good gift.

As for the inside, it has 256GB of storage but is upgradeable to a maximum of 1TB. It's powered by the 2023 A17 Bionic chip that Apple uses exclusively on iPhone 15 Pro models. It's the fastest mobile processor currently available. And just like the iPhone 13, it has an IP68 rating, making it splash, water, and dust resistant.

This fancy beast has an OLED 6.7-inch Super Retina XDR display with a Dynamic Island and ProMotion support. The adaptive ProMotion technology allows the refresh rates to reach up to 120Hz. This screen supports HDR content, True Tone technology for warmer and more accurate colors, and has a 2796 x 1290-pixel resolution at 460 pixels per inch.

Regarding the camera, this iPhone also features a TrueDepth front camera for Face ID authentication and Portrait Mode camera effects. On the back it has three lenses, and thanks to the LiDAR Scanner, it can even take Night Mode Portraits. Additional features include Deep Fusion, macro photography, Apple ProRAW, action mode, 25x digital zoom, and cinematic video shooting.

Best Apple products to gift: The bottom line

The holiday season is a great time to share precious moments with those we care about. While human connections are priceless, and no gift can express to what extent we love those around us, exchanged gestures are always a nice reminder of these positive and healthy relationships we have. Apple products are some of the best options out there to share the joy around.

These products are premium, durable, and have high reselling values down the road. My favorite is the Apple Gift Card because it ensures the recipient will find a proper use for it. While most people will value a present anyway, it's a plus knowing that they actually use it. $25 at Amazon $25 at Apple

The holiday season is right around the corner, and now's the time to start buying your loved ones some gifts. Apple products happen to be a prevalent option among many, due to their premium status, popularity, and high resale value. Whether you're seeking one of the latest iPhones, a versatile iPad, a capable Mac, or something else, we've got you covered. We will be highlighting below some of the best Apple product deals that act as solid holiday season gifts.

  • Apple Gift Card
    Best overall
    $25 at Amazon
  • Apple AirTag
    Best for affordability
    $29 at Amazon
  • Apple Watch Series 9
    Best for parents
    $390 at Amazon
  • MacBook Air (M2)
    Best for some special: Productivity
    $949 at Best Buy (13 inches)
  • Apple AirPods Pro 2
    Best for young adults
    $230 at Amazon

    $599 at Apple

    Best for students
    $399 at Amazon
  • Source: Apple

    $1100 at Best Buy

Best Apple products to gift

Apple Gift Card
Best overall

Versatile and convenient

The Apple Gift Card is a versatile present that can cost as little as $25. Your friends and family will be able to spend it however they want inside the Apple ecosystem, whether on hardware or software.

Pros
  • Flexible pricing
  • Eco-friendly
  • Supports hardware and software purchases
Cons
  • Apple ID and Gift Card regions must match
  • May be perceived as a boring gift
  • Not as personal as other options
$25 at Amazon $25 at Apple

If you're not sure what your friend or family member likes, but you know they're in the Apple ecosystem, an Apple Gift Card can act as a flexible present. You can gift somebody $25-$200 in Apple credits — between $25, $50, $75, $100, $150, $200, or a custom amount, and you also get to select the number of cards. So, if you want to pay someone more than $200, you could send multiple ones.

You can add a personalized message to your card, so it's not just a generic email when it reaches their inbox. Apple also allows you to choose one of several different patterns, so you can match their vibe. What's great about this digital gift is that you can schedule it beforehand, and it'll arrive whenever you want it to. So if you tend to be forgetful during this season, you can buy it right now and have it delivered to them on Christmas.

So what can they do with Apple credits once they redeem them? Anything related to Apple, whether it be Apple hardware or digital goods like apps, games, subscriptions, and more. The credits also work in both physical Apple Stores and the online one. Though, make sure their Apple ID is based in the same region as the gift card. If you buy a U.S. Apple Gift Card and send it to a foreign friend abroad, it won't work with their non-U.S. Apple ID.

Apple Gift Cards are truly the best for their versatility. Your beloved ones can use them however they want, and you won't have to worry about spending too much if you're on a limited budget. They're also eco-friendly, considering they're delivered as an email and don't go through production and packaging processes.

Apple AirTag
Best for affordability

Small yet effective

Apple's AirTag offers a low-cost, reliable solution when you need to track an object. It integrates into the Find My network, which allows it to be detected by millions of other Apple devices.

Pros
  • Find My network integration
  • Long battery life
  • Affordable
Cons
  • Keychain sold separately
  • Can't pair with Android
  • Scratches easily
$29 at Amazon $29 at Apple $29 at Best Buy

An Apple AirTag is the best affordable, useful Apple gift. It costs a mere $29, and its replaceable battery lasts for a whole year. It's the perfect gift to buy for a distant relative or friend with whom you want to share the joy of the season. An AirTag is also a great option if you're somebody who can't afford to buy your loved ones more expensive gifts.

With Find My Network support, an AirTag can be found if a compatible Apple device passes next to it. So it has better coverage than rivals, such as Tile. AirTags can be used to find anything and everything you might lose. Though, you will need an iPhone or iPad to set it up. So avoid gifting it to Android users.

The best part about AirTags is that you can never have enough of them. They can be attached to keys, backpacks, luggage, wallets, or even cars. Numerous people have found their stolen cars thanks to it, and many users utilize it to remember their parking spots. Unfortunately, though, you will need to separately buy a keychain or case to attach them to objects. Furthermore, their exterior shows scratches quite easily.

These coin-sized tracking devices only come in one color, but there are plenty of colorful attachment accessories that can make them look more fun. You can also craft a keychain accessory to go with your AirTag gift, making it more personal. AirTags also come in a pack of four for a discounted $99. So you can get people more than one if you have a higher budget.

Apple Watch Series 9
Best for parents

24/7 health and fitness monitoring

$390 $399 Save $9

Apple's Watch Series 9 packs in a new, more powerful processor, a brighter display capable of up to 2,000 nits peak brightness, Apple's U2 chip that enables precision-finding of your iPhone, as well as better HomePod integration, and a whole slew of features and tech you'd expect.

Pros
  • Tracks a wide range of health and workout metrics
  • Supports calls, texts, music playback, and more
  • Offers plenty of color options
Cons
  • Mediocre battery life
  • Inferior sleep tracking
  • Requires an iPhone to set up
$390 at Amazon $399 at Best Buy $399 at Apple

Whether it's a parent or a grandparent, the Apple Watch Series 9 is the best gift for them. This smartwatch was released back in September, so it's still a fresh, new product, and there's a lot to like.

The watch is available in so many configurations, so you will likely find at least one combo that matches their style. They'll also get access to a number of health features. So if you're concerned about their health, getting them an Apple Watch would be a great idea. There's a reason why the Apple Watch is the best-selling smartwatch right now. The technology it packs in its compact body is unrivaled.

The Apple Watch Series 9 supports:

  • Blood oxygen measurements
  • ECGs
  • Walking steadiness notifications
  • Cardio fitness level notifications
  • Irregular heart rhythm notifications
  • High & low heart rate notifications
  • Fall detection
  • Emergency SOS
  • Medical ID
  • Loud noise notifications
  • Crash detection
  • Sleep tracking (albeit not very accurate)

If they don't own an iPhone for setup, you don't have to worry about that either. The GPS + Cellular model of the Apple Watch Series 9 can be set up through your iPhone. Though, if neither you nor any of their family members uses an iPhone, then they won't be able to set it up at all.

The health features on this watch aren't solely what makes it so great. If your parent tends to miss calls or notifications, the Apple Watch will make sure this never happens again. Not only does it vibrate on their wrist, but it also can play optional notification sounds. They'll never miss a call or text as long as they wear it. They can even answer calls right from their wrist, so if the phone isn't nearby, they can answer without moving an inch. They can also ask Siri to send texts, so they don't even have to type or click buttons.

The Apple Watch Series 9 starts at $399, which makes it a good gift that's not way too expensive. It also comes with a three-month Apple Fitness Plus trial, so your parent can have somebody guide them through workouts — even ones that are targeted at older adults.

MacBook Air (M2)
Best for some special: Productivity

The best MacBook Air yet

$949 $1099 Save $150

The latest MacBook Air offers the M2 chip, and a redesigned chassis with MagSafe 3 support. It is powerful and can handle pretty much any kind of work, and it comes in either 13.6-inch or 15.3-inch sizes.

Pros
  • Lightweight, sleek design
  • Long battery life
  • Capable M2 chip
Cons
  • Limited port options
  • Somewhat expensive
  • Dull finishes
$949 at Best Buy (13 inches) $1049 at Best Buy (15 inches) $1080 at Amazon (13 inches) $1099 at Amazon (15 inches)

If you're looking to buy a special somebody an expensive gift, you may want to consider a MacBook Air M2. It was released in 2022, making it the latest and most powerful MacBook Air to date. The base model starts at $1,099, so it's not the cheapest holiday gift. But if you want to spend a notable amount on a productivity device for someone you love, you're in the right place.

The MacBook Air M2 offers a fresh exterior design that introduces a notched display in the process. It features two USB-C ports, a MagSafe 3 port, and a 3.5mm headphone jack. I'm personally a big fan of its design because it's clean, slim, and modern, and it comes in four aluminum finishes: Midnight, Starlight, Space Gray, and Silver. While the four colors are somewhat dull, you could always protect it with a case or throw a skin on it. It's still the greatest Apple device for lightweight productivity.

This MacBook starts with 8GB of RAM and a 256GB SSD. As the name suggests, it features the M2 processor, so you'll get every single new feature of the new macOS Sonoma operating system.

Whether your loved one is a student or someone who works in a field that doesn't require ginormous computing powers, this MacBook will handle their everyday tasks smoothly. It's also safe to assume that it will receive software updates for at least four more years, based on Apple's history. In a nutshell, it's a capable, future-proof device that your beloved will appreciate.

Apple AirPods Pro 2
Best for young adults

A must-have for iPhone users

$230 $249 Save $19

Despite their compact build, the AirPods Pro 2 pack in plenty of features, including active noise cancelation (ANC), Dolby Atmos content, and wireless charging.

Pros
  • Effective ANC
  • Find My integration
  • Spatial Audio support
Cons
  • No companion app on Android
  • Case scratches easily
  • Not the cheapest earbuds
$230 at Amazon $250 at Best Buy $249 at Apple

The AirPods have always been great earbuds, but the AirPods Pro 2 are my favorite of the whole lineup. These true wireless earbuds from Apple offer active noise cancelation (ANC), a feature I use almost daily. Whether you're working at a café, commuting, or merely jogging, ANC really helps you hone in on whatever you're listening to without having to worry about outside noise. There are other AirPods on the market, but the regular models don't support ANC and the AirPods Max are over-ear, which some people are not fans of.

Apple's AirPods Pro 2 also support Spatial Audio with dynamic head tracking. So you get to listen to music the way the artist visualized it when it was getting recorded. This feature is available on Apple Music and some other third-party services. It also works with the Apple TV app, so you can become part of the (supported) movies and series you're watching.

These wireless earbuds are water and sweat-resistant, so you can work out with them. Apple doesn't cover water damage under its limited warranty, so be careful when you're jogging during rainy days. To ensure these little buds don't fall out of your ears when running, check the other ear tip sizes included in the package. You can also do a fit test from your iPhone to make sure you've chosen the correct size.

Apple offers optional engraving when purchasing AirPods from its online store. So if you want to add a unique touch to make your gift more personal, you can do that for free. They're available for $249 in white color only. Their charging case supports Qi and MagSafe wireless chargers, as well as the Apple Watch charging puck.

Apple iPhone 13
Best for someone special: Medium budget

Solid and doesn't cost a fortune

The iPhone 13 is solid for those on a limited budget, offering a dual camera system, A15 Bionic chip, and vibrant color options.

Pros
  • Supports the latest iOS 17
  • Packs the capable A15 Bionic
  • Vibrant finishes
Cons
  • No Dynamic Island
  • 60Hz screen, no AOD
  • Lightning port
$599 at Apple $630 at Best Buy

If you have an extra special person in your life but don't want to spend over a grand, you might want to consider an iPhone 13. This iPhone starts at $599, so it's neither the cheapest nor the most expensive out there. This is because it was released in 2021, so it's not from the latest lineup, but with that age comes that discount.

The iPhone 13 is available in six different finishes: Red, Starlight, Midnight, Blue, Green, and Pink. The base model has 128GB of storage, but it can be upgraded to up to 512GB. Despite being older, it's still powered by Apple's A15 Bionic chip, which is one of the fastest you can get on a smartphone today. It's also the same processor included in the regular iPhone 14 models.

This iPhone has an OLED 6.1-inch Super Retina XDR display for easy carry and a 2532 x 1170 resolution at 460 pixels per inch for vivid, accurate colors. Its display also supports True Tone technology, which adds a warm layer to the colors depending on the surrounding lighting. This makes the colors look more natural and as they're meant to be. This iPhone also has splash, water, and dust resistance, rated at IP68, so it can last at a maximum depth of 6 meters for 30 minutes. However, it's worth reminding you that Apple's limited warranty doesn't cover water damage.

When it comes to the front camera, the iPhone 13 supports Face ID and Portrait Mode camera effects, thanks to the TrueDepth camera system. The rear bump includes two camera lenses that support 4K video recording, Cinematic Mode, Night Mode shooting, and more.

In a nutshell, the iPhone 13 is the best gift to get someone who is exceptionally special to you. It might not be the latest and greatest iPhone, but it'll save you some money and make your loved one feel great this holiday.

Apple iPad 10
Best for students

A low-cost, premium tablet

$399 $449 Save $50

The iPad 10 introduces a complete chassis overhaul and offers four bold colors. It also packs the A14 Bionic chip and supports the Apple Pencil 1 and USB-C.

Pros
  • Supports the latest iPadOS 17
  • USB-C port
  • Sleek design
Cons
  • No Apple Pencil 2 support
  • Dated A14 Bionic processor
  • 60Hz screen
$399 at Amazon $449 at Best Buy $449 at Apple

The affordable iPad 10 was released back in 2022, and it's most suitable for people who don't require a Mac, such as students. While its power is somewhat limited, considering it's a mobile device, iPadOS 17 still makes it a solid productivity tool. It starts at $449 and is available in four finishes: Blue, Pink, Yellow, and Silver.

The iPad 10 comes with a sleek design similar to that of higher-end models. It features thin front bezels without a physical home button. It's a slim, lightweight device that anyone can just slip into their backpack and carry on the go. So whether you're gifting it to a high school or college student, it'll be a decent tablet for paperless education. It's also great for older adults who might not need a dedicated laptop.

The iPad 10 is excellent for attending online classes, taking notes, reading eBooks, listening to podcasts, and watching TV content. It has a 10.9-inch display that supports Apple Pencil 1 and Apple Pencil (USB-C) for drawing or taking notes. The best part? Apple's Notes app recognizes handwriting, so students can search through their notes as if they've been typed on a keyboard.

The front 12MP ultra-wide camera on the iPad has a 122-degree field of view. So if they're moving around while attending a class or a regular video call, the camera will follow them around. However, it's worth mentioning that this camera doesn't support Portrait Mode camera effects or Face ID, due to the lack of a TrueDepth camera system.

Source: Apple
Apple iPhone 15 Pro Max
Best for someone special: Generous edition

The latest and greatest

The iPhone 15 Pro Max introduces an all-new, customizable Action button, a titanium chassis with round edges, and a USB Type-C port. It exclusively offers up to 5x optical zoom, too.

Pros
  • USB-C port
  • Light, titanium build
  • Action button, Dynamic Island, AOD
Cons
  • Costly
  • Dull colorways
  • Too large for some users
$1100 at Best Buy $1200 at Amazon $1199 at Apple

This one is for all of you extra-generous peeps who are looking to buy something fancy. The iPhone 15 Pro Max starts at $1,199 for the base model, making it the most expensive gift in our guide. However, this is the perfect gift if you want to spend a significant amount of money on your loved one or want to get them the latest, highest-end device.

This iPhone comes in four finishes: Black Titanium, White Titanium, Blue Titanium, and Natural Titanium. It has a Ceramic Shield front, textured matte glass back, and a titanium design that makes it look as premium and solid as it gets. While it's one of the most durable iPhones manufactured in the past years, it still features glass fronts and backs, so certain drops might crack them. Repairing the front or back of an iPhone 15 Pro Max costs a fortune, so you may want to invest in a case to minimize potential damage. It would also make for another good gift.

As for the inside, it has 256GB of storage but is upgradeable to a maximum of 1TB. It's powered by the 2023 A17 Bionic chip that Apple uses exclusively on iPhone 15 Pro models. It's the fastest mobile processor currently available. And just like the iPhone 13, it has an IP68 rating, making it splash, water, and dust resistant.

This fancy beast has an OLED 6.7-inch Super Retina XDR display with a Dynamic Island and ProMotion support. The adaptive ProMotion technology allows the refresh rates to reach up to 120Hz. This screen supports HDR content, True Tone technology for warmer and more accurate colors, and has a 2796 x 1290-pixel resolution at 460 pixels per inch.

Regarding the camera, this iPhone also features a TrueDepth front camera for Face ID authentication and Portrait Mode camera effects. On the back it has three lenses, and thanks to the LiDAR Scanner, it can even take Night Mode Portraits. Additional features include Deep Fusion, macro photography, Apple ProRAW, action mode, 25x digital zoom, and cinematic video shooting.

Best Apple products to gift: The bottom line

The holiday season is a great time to share precious moments with those we care about. While human connections are priceless, and no gift can express to what extent we love those around us, exchanged gestures are always a nice reminder of these positive and healthy relationships we have. Apple products are some of the best options out there to share the joy around.

These products are premium, durable, and have high reselling values down the road. My favorite is the Apple Gift Card because it ensures the recipient will find a proper use for it. While most people will value a present anyway, it's a plus knowing that they actually use it. So I'd rather give people the freedom to choose what to do with this money instead of trying to guess what they really want. If I'm aware they've added a certain item to their wishlist, though, then that's a different story.

Another reason I love digital Apple Gift Cards is that they're eco-friendly. Considering the whole process occurs online, the waste they leave behind is nonexistent compared to that of physical products. People are then free to do with their added Apple ID credit whatever suits them and their needs most.

Apple Gift Card
Best overall

The Apple Gift Card is a versatile present that can cost as little as $25. Your friends and family will be able to spend it however they want inside the Apple ecosystem, whether on hardware or software.

$25 at Amazon $25 at Apple

This feature is available on Apple Music and some other third-party services. It also works with the Apple TV app, so you can become part of the (supported) movies and series you're watching.

These wireless earbuds are water and sweat-resistant, so you can work out with them. Apple doesn't cover water damage under its limited warranty, so be careful when you're jogging during rainy days. To ensure these little buds don't fall out of your ears when running, check the other ear tip sizes included in the package. You can also do a fit test from your iPhone to make sure you've chosen the correct size.

Apple offers optional engraving when purchasing AirPods from its online store. So if you want to add a unique touch to make your gift more personal, you can do that for free. They're available for $249 in white color only. Their charging case supports Qi and MagSafe wireless chargers, as well as the Apple Watch charging puck.

Apple iPhone 13
Best for someone special: Medium budget

Solid and doesn't cost a fortune

The iPhone 13 is solid for those on a limited budget, offering a dual camera system, A15 Bionic chip, and vibrant color options.

Pros
  • Supports the latest iOS 17
  • Packs the capable A15 Bionic
  • Vibrant finishes
Cons
  • No Dynamic Island
  • 60Hz screen, no AOD
  • Lightning port
$599 at Apple $630 at Best Buy

If you have an extra special person in your life but don't want to spend over a grand, you might want to consider an iPhone 13. This iPhone starts at $599, so it's neither the cheapest nor the most expensive out there. This is because it was released in 2021, so it's not from the latest lineup, but with that age comes that discount.

The iPhone 13 is available in six different finishes: Red, Starlight, Midnight, Blue, Green, and Pink. The base model has 128GB of storage, but it can be upgraded to up to 512GB. Despite being older, it's still powered by Apple's A15 Bionic chip, which is one of the fastest you can get on a smartphone today. It's also the same processor included in the regular iPhone 14 models.

This iPhone has an OLED 6.1-inch Super Retina XDR display for easy carry and a 2532 x 1170 resolution at 460 pixels per inch for vivid, accurate colors. Its display also supports True Tone technology, which adds a warm layer to the colors depending on the surrounding lighting. This makes the colors look more natural and as they're meant to be. This iPhone also has splash, water, and dust resistance, rated at IP68, so it can last at a maximum depth of 6 meters for 30 minutes. However, it's worth reminding you that Apple's limited warranty doesn't cover water damage.

When it comes to the front camera, the iPhone 13 supports Face ID and Portrait Mode camera effects, thanks to the TrueDepth camera system. The rear bump includes two camera lenses that support 4K video recording, Cinematic Mode, Night Mode shooting, and more.

In a nutshell, the iPhone 13 is the best gift to get someone who is exceptionally special to you. It might not be the latest and greatest iPhone, but it'll save you some money and make your loved one feel great this holiday.

Apple iPad 10
Best for students

A low-cost, premium tablet

$399 $449 Save $50

The iPad 10 introduces a complete chassis overhaul and offers four bold colors. It also packs the A14 Bionic chip and supports the Apple Pencil 1 and USB-C.

Pros
  • Supports the latest iPadOS 17
  • USB-C port
  • Sleek design
Cons
  • No Apple Pencil 2 support
  • Dated A14 Bionic processor
  • 60Hz screen
$399 at Amazon $449 at Best Buy $449 at Apple

The affordable iPad 10 was released back in 2022, and it's most suitable for people who don't require a Mac, such as students. While its power is somewhat limited, considering it's a mobile device, iPadOS 17 still makes it a solid productivity tool. It starts at $449 and is available in four finishes: Blue, Pink, Yellow, and Silver.

The iPad 10 comes with a sleek design similar to that of higher-end models. It features thin front bezels without a physical home button. It's a slim, lightweight device that anyone can just slip into their backpack and carry on the go. So whether you're gifting it to a high school or college student, it'll be a decent tablet for paperless education. It's also great for older adults who might not need a dedicated laptop.

The iPad 10 is excellent for attending online classes, taking notes, reading eBooks, listening to podcasts, and watching TV content. It has a 10.9-inch display that supports Apple Pencil 1 and Apple Pencil (USB-C) for drawing or taking notes. The best part? Apple's Notes app recognizes handwriting, so students can search through their notes as if they've been typed on a keyboard.

The front 12MP ultra-wide camera on the iPad has a 122-degree field of view. So if they're moving around while attending a class or a regular video call, the camera will follow them around. However, it's worth mentioning that this camera doesn't support Portrait Mode camera effects or Face ID, due to the lack of a TrueDepth camera system.

Source: Apple
Apple iPhone 15 Pro Max
Best for someone special: Generous edition

The latest and greatest

The iPhone 15 Pro Max introduces an all-new, customizable Action button, a titanium chassis with round edges, and a USB Type-C port. It exclusively offers up to 5x optical zoom, too.

Pros
  • USB-C port
  • Light, titanium build
  • Action button, Dynamic Island, AOD
Cons
  • Costly
  • Dull colorways
  • Too large for some users
$1100 at Best Buy $1200 at Amazon $1199 at Apple

This one is for all of you extra-generous peeps who are looking to buy something fancy. The iPhone 15 Pro Max starts at $1,199 for the base model, making it the most expensive gift in our guide. However, this is the perfect gift if you want to spend a significant amount of money on your loved one or want to get them the latest, highest-end device.

This iPhone comes in four finishes: Black Titanium, White Titanium, Blue Titanium, and Natural Titanium. It has a Ceramic Shield front, textured matte glass back, and a titanium design that makes it look as premium and solid as it gets. While it's one of the most durable iPhones manufactured in the past years, it still features glass fronts and backs, so certain drops might crack them. Repairing the front or back of an iPhone 15 Pro Max costs a fortune, so you may want to invest in a case to minimize potential damage. It would also make for another good gift.

As for the inside, it has 256GB of storage but is upgradeable to a maximum of 1TB. It's powered by the 2023 A17 Bionic chip that Apple uses exclusively on iPhone 15 Pro models. It's the fastest mobile processor currently available. And just like the iPhone 13, it has an IP68 rating, making it splash, water, and dust resistant.

This fancy beast has an OLED 6.7-inch Super Retina XDR display with a Dynamic Island and ProMotion support. The adaptive ProMotion technology allows the refresh rates to reach up to 120Hz. This screen supports HDR content, True Tone technology for warmer and more accurate colors, and has a 2796 x 1290-pixel resolution at 460 pixels per inch.

Regarding the camera, this iPhone also features a TrueDepth front camera for Face ID authentication and Portrait Mode camera effects. On the back it has three lenses, and thanks to the LiDAR Scanner, it can even take Night Mode Portraits. Additional features include Deep Fusion, macro photography, Apple ProRAW, action mode, 25x digital zoom, and cinematic video shooting.

Best Apple products to gift: The bottom line

The holiday season is a great time to share precious moments with those we care about. While human connections are priceless, and no gift can express to what extent we love those around us, exchanged gestures are always a nice reminder of these positive and healthy relationships we have. Apple products are some of the best options out there to share the joy around.

These products are premium, durable, and have high reselling values down the road. My favorite is the Apple Gift Card because it ensures the recipient will find a proper use for it. While most people will value a present anyway, it's a plus knowing that they actually use it. So I'd rather give people the freedom to choose what to do with this money instead of trying to guess what they really want. If I'm aware they've added a certain item to their wishlist, though, then that's a different story.

Another reason I love digital Apple Gift Cards is that they're eco-friendly. Considering the whole process occurs online, the waste they leave behind is nonexistent compared to that of physical products. People are then free to do with their added Apple ID credit whatever suits them and their needs most.

Apple Gift Card
Best overall

The Apple Gift Card is a versatile present that can cost as little as $25. Your friends and family will be able to spend it however they want inside the Apple ecosystem, whether on hardware or software.

$25 at Amazon $25 at Apple

item.title: ChatGPT creator OpenAI fires CEO Sam Altman
item.itunes:title: ChatGPT creator OpenAI fires CEO Sam Altman
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • Sam Altman was fired today as CEO by the board of directors due to a loss of confidence in his ability to lead the company.
  • Mira Murati will serve as interim CEO while a permanent replacement is sought.
  • Altman's departure follows a review process by the board, which concluded that he lacked consistent candid communication with the board's members.

Sam Altman, a co-founder of OpenAI, was fired from his position as CEO of the nonprofit by its board of directors Friday. The stunning move comes after OpenAI's board lost confidence in Altman's ability to lead the company. In the interm, Mira Murati will serve as CEO of OpenAI while the board searches for a permanent replacement. Murati was previously the chief technology officer at OpenAI, and was part of the nonprofit for five years before her ascension to the interim CEO position.

"Mr. Altman’s departure follows a deliberative review process by the board, which concluded that he was not consistently candid in his communications with the board, hindering its ability to exercise its responsibilities," the company said in a press release. "The board no longer has confidence in his ability to continue leading OpenAI."

"I loved my time at OpenAI," said Altman in a post on X (formerly Twitter) following the announcement. "It was transformative for me personally, and hopefully the world a little bit. "Most of all, I loved working with such talented people. Will have more to say about what’s next later."

Though Altman will no longer be leading OpenAI, he still has far-reaching connections and influence in the artificial intelligence space. Altman is single-handedly the largest shareholder in Humane, a company made up of a few ex-Apple employees. Humane just launched its first product, the Humane AI Pin. As the name suggests, this new device is all about artificial intelligence, and even makes use of OpenAI's GPT-4 language model.

What's next for OpenAI

OpenAI is the nonprofit organization that oversees the company's projects, including ChatGPT and other artificial intelligence language models. The leadership transition comes as a shock, since Altman has appeared on behalf of OpenAI in public capacities as recently as earlier this month. OpenAI held its first-ever developer conference in San Francisco starting Nov. 6, called DevDay, with Altman at the helm.

Now, Altman has been fired from his position as CEO of OpenAI and was removed from the company's board of directors. In a corresponding move, Greg Brockman is stepping down as chairman of OpenAI's board of directors. However, Brockman will remain in his position as president, reporting to interm CEO Murati. It's unclear what impact, if any, this leadership shuffle will have on OpenAI's day-to-day operations. Based on Altman's public presence and the board's comments, it appears that these leadership changes came about fairly abruptly.

OpenAI has a unique makeup, as it is a nonprofit but has a structure that allows for capital investments from for-profit companies. In the release, OpenAI's board reiterated the company's mission. "OpenAI was deliberately structured to advance our mission: to ensure that artificial general intelligence benefits all humanity," the board said in a statement. "We are grateful for Sam’s many contributions to the founding and growth of OpenAI. At the same time, we believe new leadership is necessary as we move forward."

OpenAI is an industry-leader in the artificial intelligence boom, and has backers that include Microsoft. Its chatbot, ChatGPT has seen immense success, to the point that it recently paused new ChatGPT Plus subscriptions due to overwhelming demand. It's also one of the best AI chatbots. Amidst this success, it will now undergo a leadership transition, and is currently searching for a permanent replacement for Altman.

item.title: Walmart Black Friday 2023 Deals: Save big on TVs, tablets, and headphones
item.itunes:title: Walmart Black Friday 2023 Deals: Save big on TVs, tablets, and headphones
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Quick Links

  • TVs
  • Tablets
  • Headphones

As the weather gets colder, Walmart’s early Black Friday deals are only getting hotter with savings on electronics. You can, for example, upgrade your living room with a massive new TV to make the most of the rest of football season. You could even pick up and outdoor TV that’s perfect for the back patio when you’re sitting by the fire pit. You can also save on Bluetooth headphones and tablets which can make long flights feel shorter and bumpy car rides more enjoyable.

Walmart isn't the only retailer with early Black Friday deals. Best Buy's Black Friday deals are also live with extra savings for My Best Buy members as well.

TVs

Crowding around the TV for holiday movies with the family or binge-watching a Netflix series is a lot more enjoyable with a modern 4K TV with support for HDR and built-in apps. These TVs support modern HDR content for better colors and also have apps built-in, so you don’t need to juggle multiple remotes. An OLED TV, like LG's C2 series, can be a big leap forward from your old TV with perfect blacks and vibrant colors. While an OLED TV is more expensive than an LED model, these deals can make the price a lot more palatable.

Source: Walmart
Onn 65-inch 4K LED Roku Smart TV
$298 $348 Save $50

This 65-inch TV from Onn is a great size for watching sports during the day and putting on a movie at night. It has a 4K resolution with HDR10+ support, so your shows can look sharp and vibrant. It’s also easy to use with the simple Roku interface and plenty of apps.

$298 at Walmart
Samsung The Terrace 65-inch Outdoor 4K QLED TV
$2990 $4999 Save $2009

This IP55 rated TV is designed to be used outdoors, so you can watch your shows from the back patio. This is excellent for the summer, but can also be the perfect for providing entertainment while you’re sitting around a fire pit.

$2990 at Walmart
Source: LG
LG C2 65-inch 4K OLED TV
$1399 $1900 Save $501

The LG C2 OLED has delivers excellent colors and black levels with a stunning 4K resolution thanks to it’s OLEd panel. It runs LG’s WebOS software, so it’s ready to download your favorite apps as soon as you get it peered on.

$1399 at Walmart
  • SAMSUNG 55" Class LS03B The Frame QLED 4K Smart TV (Save $400)
  • LG 77" Class 4K UHD OLED Web OS Smart TV with Dolby Vision C2 Series (save $677)
  • SAMSUNG 65" Class S90C OLED 4K Smart TV (Save $1702)

Tablets

If you’ve been thinking about a new tablet this winter season, Walmart has some deals to help you save including Apple Deals on iPad. If you’re traveling, a tablet can be a quick and easy way to check your email or to browse the web in a waiting room. Tablets can also help with work with enough room on the screen to read a spreadsheet or to see your team members on a video call. You could also get one purely for entertainment, so you can stream and game without running down the battery on your phone.

Source: Samsung
Samsung Galaxy Tab A8
$179 $230 Save $51

The Samsung Galaxy Tab A8 is an entry-level tablet with a 10.5-inch 1920x1200 display and a large battery. While it only comes with 32GB of RAM, it can take an SD card up, so you can keep downloading.

$230 at Amazon $179 at Walmart
Source: Apple
Apple iPad 9th Gen (2021)
$249 $329 Save $80

Apple's iPad really needs no introduction with an excellent design that's easy to hold, a vibrant display, and some of the best apps you can get on a tablet. This model comes with 64GB of storage, but the 128GB version is also on sale.

$249 at Walmart
Apple iPad 10
$399 $449 Save $50

If you want an iPad with a newer design, this 10th generation iPad has a fast A14 Bionic CPU, USB-C charging, and of course, access to Apple's vast app store. You can get silver or blue colors at this price.

$399 at Walmart
  • SAMSUNG Galaxy Tab S8+, 12.4" Tablet 128GB (Wi-Fi), S Pen Included (Save $183)
  • 2021 Apple 11-inch iPad Pro Wi-Fi 2TB (Save $650)

Headphones

Wireless headphones are an excellent addition to your daily carry and are the perfect accessory for a cross-country flight or a long journey on the train. Over-ear headphones reduce ambient noise on their own thanks to thick padding, but those with active noise-canceling like the Sony WH-1000XM4 can get reduce even more noise like the drone of jet engines on a plane or the wind noise as a car passenger. One of the best over-ear headphones you can get, the Sony WH-1000XM5 headphones are also on sale.

Source: Sony
Sony WH-1000XM4
$250 $348 Save $98

Sony’s WH-X1000XM series has gained almost legendary status thanks to its excellent noise cancelation and overall high-quality audio. These Bluetooth headphones come with an aux cable, so you can use them wired, as well as a hard carrying case.

$250 at Walmart
Sony WH-1000XM5
$325 $400 Save $75

With an updated design and some new components inside, these are some of the best headphones you can buy. They look nicer than the previous generation without giving up any battery capacity or features, and even features one upgrades when it comes to noise-canceling. You can also pick these up in silver for the same price.

$325 at Walmart

This IP55 rated TV is designed to be used outdoors, so you can watch your shows from the back patio. This is excellent for the summer, but can also be the perfect for providing entertainment while you’re sitting around a fire pit.

$2990 at Walmart
Source: LG
LG C2 65-inch 4K OLED TV
$1399 $1900 Save $501

The LG C2 OLED has delivers excellent colors and black levels with a stunning 4K resolution thanks to it’s OLEd panel. It runs LG’s WebOS software, so it’s ready to download your favorite apps as soon as you get it peered on.

$1399 at Walmart
  • SAMSUNG 55" Class LS03B The Frame QLED 4K Smart TV (Save $400)
  • LG 77" Class 4K UHD OLED Web OS Smart TV with Dolby Vision C2 Series (save $677)
  • SAMSUNG 65" Class S90C OLED 4K Smart TV (Save $1702)

Tablets

If you’ve been thinking about a new tablet this winter season, Walmart has some deals to help you save including Apple Deals on iPad. If you’re traveling, a tablet can be a quick and easy way to check your email or to browse the web in a waiting room. Tablets can also help with work with enough room on the screen to read a spreadsheet or to see your team members on a video call. You could also get one purely for entertainment, so you can stream and game without running down the battery on your phone.

Source: Samsung
Samsung Galaxy Tab A8
$179 $230 Save $51

The Samsung Galaxy Tab A8 is an entry-level tablet with a 10.5-inch 1920x1200 display and a large battery. While it only comes with 32GB of RAM, it can take an SD card up, so you can keep downloading.

$230 at Amazon $179 at Walmart
Source: Apple
Apple iPad 9th Gen (2021)
$249 $329 Save $80

Apple's iPad really needs no introduction with an excellent design that's easy to hold, a vibrant display, and some of the best apps you can get on a tablet. This model comes with 64GB of storage, but the 128GB version is also on sale.

$249 at Walmart
Apple iPad 10
$399 $449 Save $50

If you want an iPad with a newer design, this 10th generation iPad has a fast A14 Bionic CPU, USB-C charging, and of course, access to Apple's vast app store. You can get silver or blue colors at this price.

$399 at Walmart
  • SAMSUNG Galaxy Tab S8+, 12.4" Tablet 128GB (Wi-Fi), S Pen Included (Save $183)
  • 2021 Apple 11-inch iPad Pro Wi-Fi 2TB (Save $650)

Headphones

Wireless headphones are an excellent addition to your daily carry and are the perfect accessory for a cross-country flight or a long journey on the train. Over-ear headphones reduce ambient noise on their own thanks to thick padding, but those with active noise-canceling like the Sony WH-1000XM4 can get reduce even more noise like the drone of jet engines on a plane or the wind noise as a car passenger. One of the best over-ear headphones you can get, the Sony WH-1000XM5 headphones are also on sale.

Source: Sony
Sony WH-1000XM4
$250 $348 Save $98

Sony’s WH-X1000XM series has gained almost legendary status thanks to its excellent noise cancelation and overall high-quality audio. These Bluetooth headphones come with an aux cable, so you can use them wired, as well as a hard carrying case.

$250 at Walmart
Sony WH-1000XM5
$325 $400 Save $75

With an updated design and some new components inside, these are some of the best headphones you can buy. They look nicer than the previous generation without giving up any battery capacity or features, and even features one upgrades when it comes to noise-canceling. You can also pick these up in silver for the same price.

$325 at Walmart

item.title: AMD's hybrid APUs are finally official, but they're not moving the needle much
item.itunes:title: AMD's hybrid APUs are finally official, but they're not moving the needle much
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • AMD's hybrid approach to CPUs differs from Intel's, with their Phoenix 2 APU not shaking things up as much as Intel's Alder Lake. The real benefit for AMD is in manufacturing, allowing for smaller and cheaper processors.
  • Phoenix 2, AMD's hybrid APU, is similar to its predecessor but with fewer CPU and GPU cores. It is built on the same process and architecture, with slight differences in cache and features.
  • AMD's choice of a single-CCX design for Phoenix 2 improves core-to-core latencies. The ratio of regular Zen cores to dense Zen cores is likely to remain 1:2 for some time, as AMD may not introduce a new CCX design until a few generations later.

It was only recently that AMD finally made the launch of its first hybrid processor, colloquially (but not officially) named Phoenix 2. This APU features two regular Zen 4 cores and four area- and power-efficient Zen 4c cores, for a grand total of six cores. Intel beat AMD to the punch with hybrid architecture, with Lakefield in 2020 as a proof-of-concept and Alder Lake in 2021 as the real deal. Now, AMD has caught up to its rival and will be making hybrid processors for the foreseeable future.

The thing is, AMD's approach to hybrid CPUs is very different from Intel's, and on a per-core basis they're not going to shake things up nearly as much as Alder Lake and Raptor Lake. Zen 4c is almost identical to Zen 4, and while there are advantages to this, ultimately it means swapping out some Zen 4 cores for 4c won't make a big difference in performance or efficiency. For AMD, the real benefit of hybrid architecture is in manufacturing, and that's the thing that might open the door for some truly new AMD CPUs.

What AMD's first hybrid processor looks like

Although AMD's hybrid APU is a different chip than the original Phoenix APU that launched earlier this year, its official codename is Phoenix. For the sake of avoiding confusion, I'll be calling this hybrid APU Phoenix 2, which is what the PC enthusiast community named it when it first leaked out earlier this year.

That being said, Phoenix 2 is basically just a smaller Phoenix and isn't totally brand-new. It has two fewer CPU cores, eight fewer GPU cores, and is physically smaller. It also lacks Ryzen AI capability and has a slightly smaller L2 cache, though that's only because it has fewer cores. But otherwise, they're built on the same TSMC 4nm process, use the same architecture, and have the same amount of L3 cache.

Phoenix

Phoenix 2

CPU Cores

8

2+4

GPU Cores

12

4

Cache

16MB L3 + 8MB L2

16MB L3 + 6MB L2

Ryzen AI

Yes

No

Die Size

178mm2

137mm2

What's particularly interesting is that Phoenix 2 is a single-CCX design. In Zen CPUs, the CCX is a group of cores and is the smallest building block, rather than individual cores. While AMD has previously made two-core, four-core, and eight-core CCXs, Phoenix 2 marks the first time AMD has made a six-core CCX, and going with one CCX means better core-to-core latencies. But that's not just an interesting tidbit, it's very crucial for the future of hybrid Zen CPUs since AMD doesn't introduce new CCX designs very often when it comes to core count.

This all means the ratio of normal Zen cores to dense Zen cores is probably going to be 1:2 for a while, since it's unlikely AMD will replace the six-core CCX until it's at least a couple of generations old. The upcoming Strix Point APU is rumored to be a 12-core chip, which means two six-core CCXs. It's highly unlikely that future APUs built with the six-core CCX will offer more than 12 cores, since more CCXs mean worse core-to-core latencies. If AMD wants to change the 1:2 core ratio or offer more cores per CCX, it will have to introduce a new CCX, but that is certainly years down the line.

How Phoenix 2 compares to Intel's hybrid CPUs

AMD has taken care to note all the differences between its hybrid designs and those of Intel's. AMD's hybrid chips will use cores that don't differ architecturally, have the same IPC, have SMT/Hyperthreading across all cores, and don't require complex scheduling. Those are all things that Intel's current Raptor Lake chips struggle with, as the company's P-cores and E-cores are architecturally different, while Zen 4 and 4c are identical. However, what Intel CPUs give up in those aspects, they gain in others, and that's just as true for AMD's hybrid APUs.

The only difference between Zen 4 and 4c in performance and efficiency is that Zen 4 can hit higher clock speeds, and that's a double-edged sword for AMD. It ultimately means that adding Zen 4c cores into the mix doesn't really change the performance or efficiency characteristics when comparing Phoenix 2 to a cut-down Phoenix chip. AMD even admits this quite plainly in its presentation about Phoenix 2, and although Phoenix 2 is more efficient than Phoenix at lower TDPs, it's a very minor difference that AMD could have accomplished with Phoenix just by tweaking the frequency per core.

Source: AMD

By contrast, Intel's P- and E-cores use different architectures to offer different power and performance profiles, with the former offering high single-threaded performance and the latter great multi-threaded performance in great numbers. The biggest tradeoff AMD is making is relying on a single core architecture to always meet its performance and efficiency needs. If Intel needs greater single-threaded performance in its next CPU, it just needs to focus on redesigning the P-cores and can simply leave the E-cores alone, for instance.

Additionally, Intel's current generation Gracemont E-cores offer a much smaller footprint and higher performance density, just like Zen 4c against Zen 4. In fact, Gracemont cores are smaller than Zen 4c cores despite being a generation behind node-wise, but of course Gracemont is much slower than Zen 4c.

It's not as simple as AMD makes it out to be with its hybrid CPU design, and Zen 4c really doesn't change much when it comes to performance and efficiency. But that's the thing, Phoenix 2 isn't really about performance and efficiency, but rather something else.

For AMD, hybrid design is about manufacturing

The key benefit of Phoenix 2 and other hybrid Ryzen APUs will be in manufacturing. Zen 4c's more compact size means smaller processors, which are obviously cheaper to manufacture than larger ones. AMD obviously wanted to develop a smaller Phoenix APU for lower-end devices, but without Zen 4c it couldn't have been so small unless it only used four Zen 4 cores, which would have resulted in much worse performance. Hybrid cores allow AMD to offer the same performance for a lower price, or to pocket the difference and make more money.

While this is a benefit Intel also gets with its approach, AMD is definitely investing far fewer resources by keeping things simple. Cost-effectiveness has been AMD's motif ever since it launched the first Zen CPUs in 2017, and its hybrid APUs continue that tradition. It will be interesting to see if AMD's approach towards hybrid design proves to be as successful as chiplets, a concept that Intel is now following with processors like Meteor Lake and Ponte Vecchio.

Additionally, we don't know if AMD is planning on bringing hybrid design to chiplet-based Ryzen CPUs. Theoretically, AMD could combine a standard eight-core Zen chiplet with a 16-core C-type Zen chiplet (which is currently exclusive to the datacenter) and easily create a 24-core CPU, which may be appealing to AMD since desktop CPUs have been stuck at 16 cores since Ryzen 3000. Such a CPU would have a triple-CCX configuration, however, and it's unclear if it would work well or even work at all. We'll all have to wait and see.

item.title: Black Friday 2023: Best Nvidia and AMD graphics card deals
item.itunes:title: Black Friday 2023: Best Nvidia and AMD graphics card deals
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Quick Links

  • Best Black Friday Nvidia GPU deals
  • Best Black Friday AMD GPU deals
  • FAQ

The market for graphics card has leveled out, and compared to the last few years there are plenty more options available at much more reasonable prices. Still, major sales events like Black Friday and Cyber Monday are a perfect opportunity to spend less than usual on big ticket items like graphics cards. You don't even need to wait for Black Friday proper, as many major retailers have already discounted a bunch of hardware in the lead-up to Black Friday and Cyber Monday.

Sure, you can pick up a full pre-built system from our roundup of the best Black Friday computer and laptop deals, but those looking to upgrade their current build or start something entirely new will want to check out what's on offer here. We've collected the best Black Friday deals on AMD and Nvidia GPUs to help you land the perfect graphics card for your PC.

Best Black Friday Nvidia GPU deals

  • Source: Asus
    ASUS TUF GeForce GTX 1660 Super
    $198 $330 Save $132

    The Asus TUF GTX 1660 Super is a great starter option for people just getting into PC gaming. Its 6GB of VRAM and 1,408 CUDA cores can handle a quality and affordable 1080p gaming experience.

    $198 at Amazon
  • Source: MSI
    MSI Ventus 3X GeForce RTX 4070
    $550 $600 Save $50

    Newegg is offering $20 off this GPU with a rebate card, and you can save an extra $30 by using promo code ABFCYA456 at checkout. The RTX 4070 has 12GB of VRAM and a triple-fan setup for a great mid- to high-range gaming experience.

    $550 at Newegg
  • Source: Gigabyte
    Gigabyte Nvidia GeForce RTX 4080 Eagle OC
    $1060 $1200 Save $140

    Gigabyte's Nvidia RTX 4080 Eagle with 16GB of VRAM and triple-fan setup is discounted by $140 as part of Best Buy's early Black Friday sales event.

    $1060 at Best Buy

More great Nvidia GPU deals ahead of Black Friday

We're already seeing plenty more great Black Friday deals on Nvidia GPUs. We've curated this list to include the latest discounts, and we'll keep it updated as we approach Black Friday.

  • Source: Gigabyte
    Gigabyte GeForce RTX 4080 Gaming OC
    $1250 $1350 Save $100
    $1250 at Best Buy
  • Source: MSI
    MSI Ventus 3X GeForce RTX 4070 Ti
    $750 $800 Save $50
    $750 at Newegg
  • Source: Gigabyte
    Gigabyte GeForce RTX 4060 Ti Gaming OC 8G

    Use code ABFCYA477 at checkout for full discount

    $380 $430 Save $50
    $380 at Newegg

Best Black Friday AMD GPU deals

  • Source: XFX
    XFX Speedster SWFT 319 AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT CORE
    $400 $460 Save $60

    The XFX Speedster RX 6800 GPU is from the last generation, but considering it's just $400 you probably won't mind. I still use an RX 6800 for a quality 1440p gaming experience.

    $400 at Best Buy $400 at Amazon
  • Source: Gigabyte
    Gigabyte Gaming OC Radeon RX 7900 XT
    $900 $1000 Save $100

    Gigabyte's Gaming OC Radeon RX 7900 XT is an awesome GPU for 1440p gaming, especially if you love AMD. It's currently $100 off at Best Buy.

    $900 at Best Buy
  • Source: Gigabyte
    Gigabyte Radeon RX 6600 Eagle 8G Graphics Card
    $190 $270 Save $80

    Gigabyte's Eagle RX 6600 GPU is an affordable card that can handle a quality 1080p gaming experience. Newegg has it discounted by 29%, bringing the total down to just $190.

    $190 at Newegg

FAQ

Q: Is Black Friday a good time to buy a new Nvidia or AMD graphics card?

Black Friday and Cyber Monday — which at this point are essentially the same thing — are a great opportunity to save on big ticket items like laptops, desktop PCs, TVs, phones, and graphics cards. GPUs from every performance range seem to go on sale, and practically anyone can save. If you're looking for a GPU for gaming, going with the most expensive you can afford is usually recommended, at least as long as your processor can match its performance to avoid bottlenecks. We have a separate roundup of Black Friday CPU and GPU deals if you're looking to upgrade your processor as well.

Q: How can you tell if Black Friday pricing is actually worth it?

A common occurrence during Black Friday and Cyber Monday involves retailers jacking up prices in the weeks before the event to make the sales seem a lot more significant. It can be very tough to weed out these bogus deals, which is why we put together these curated lists of deals. Retailers will sometimes add a small disclaimer that notifies you about the lowest price in a certain amount of time, and there are tools like CamelCamelCamel where you can get a full pricing history at Amazon.

We've collected the best Black Friday deals on AMD and Nvidia GPUs to help you land the perfect graphics card for your PC.

Best Black Friday Nvidia GPU deals

  • Source: Asus
    ASUS TUF GeForce GTX 1660 Super
    $198 $330 Save $132

    The Asus TUF GTX 1660 Super is a great starter option for people just getting into PC gaming. Its 6GB of VRAM and 1,408 CUDA cores can handle a quality and affordable 1080p gaming experience.

    $198 at Amazon
  • Source: MSI
    MSI Ventus 3X GeForce RTX 4070
    $550 $600 Save $50

    Newegg is offering $20 off this GPU with a rebate card, and you can save an extra $30 by using promo code ABFCYA456 at checkout. The RTX 4070 has 12GB of VRAM and a triple-fan setup for a great mid- to high-range gaming experience.

    $550 at Newegg
  • Source: Gigabyte
    Gigabyte Nvidia GeForce RTX 4080 Eagle OC
    $1060 $1200 Save $140

    Gigabyte's Nvidia RTX 4080 Eagle with 16GB of VRAM and triple-fan setup is discounted by $140 as part of Best Buy's early Black Friday sales event.

    $1060 at Best Buy

More great Nvidia GPU deals ahead of Black Friday

We're already seeing plenty more great Black Friday deals on Nvidia GPUs. We've curated this list to include the latest discounts, and we'll keep it updated as we approach Black Friday.

  • Source: Gigabyte
    Gigabyte GeForce RTX 4080 Gaming OC
    $1250 $1350 Save $100
    $1250 at Best Buy
  • Source: MSI
    MSI Ventus 3X GeForce RTX 4070 Ti
    $750 $800 Save $50
    $750 at Newegg
  • Source: Gigabyte
    Gigabyte GeForce RTX 4060 Ti Gaming OC 8G

    Use code ABFCYA477 at checkout for full discount

    $380 $430 Save $50
    $380 at Newegg

Best Black Friday AMD GPU deals

  • Source: XFX
    XFX Speedster SWFT 319 AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT CORE
    $400 $460 Save $60

    The XFX Speedster RX 6800 GPU is from the last generation, but considering it's just $400 you probably won't mind. I still use an RX 6800 for a quality 1440p gaming experience.

    $400 at Best Buy $400 at Amazon
  • Source: Gigabyte
    Gigabyte Gaming OC Radeon RX 7900 XT
    $900 $1000 Save $100

    Gigabyte's Gaming OC Radeon RX 7900 XT is an awesome GPU for 1440p gaming, especially if you love AMD. It's currently $100 off at Best Buy.

    $900 at Best Buy
  • Source: Gigabyte
    Gigabyte Radeon RX 6600 Eagle 8G Graphics Card
    $190 $270 Save $80

    Gigabyte's Eagle RX 6600 GPU is an affordable card that can handle a quality 1080p gaming experience. Newegg has it discounted by 29%, bringing the total down to just $190.

    $190 at Newegg

FAQ

Q: Is Black Friday a good time to buy a new Nvidia or AMD graphics card?

Black Friday and Cyber Monday — which at this point are essentially the same thing — are a great opportunity to save on big ticket items like laptops, desktop PCs, TVs, phones, and graphics cards. GPUs from every performance range seem to go on sale, and practically anyone can save. If you're looking for a GPU for gaming, going with the most expensive you can afford is usually recommended, at least as long as your processor can match its performance to avoid bottlenecks. We have a separate roundup of Black Friday CPU and GPU deals if you're looking to upgrade your processor as well.

Q: How can you tell if Black Friday pricing is actually worth it?

A common occurrence during Black Friday and Cyber Monday involves retailers jacking up prices in the weeks before the event to make the sales seem a lot more significant. It can be very tough to weed out these bogus deals, which is why we put together these curated lists of deals. Retailers will sometimes add a small disclaimer that notifies you about the lowest price in a certain amount of time, and there are tools like CamelCamelCamel where you can get a full pricing history at Amazon.
